mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output, which drives higher corporate earnings and economic growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO momentum (3-month change) vs 20-day forward returns is negative over any 2-year rolling window or t-statistic of mean forward return in positive INDPRO months is not significantly greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8%, it indicates severe labor market slack and economic distress, which depresses consumer spending and corporate profits, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward return when UNRATE > 8% is positive or not significantly different from zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive monthly growth in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls indicates robust consumer demand and a strengthening economy, which jointly boost equity valuations through higher earnings expectations and lower recession risk.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0 and PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return following months when both RSAFS and PAYEMS increase is not positive, or the joint signal does not outperform the average of the individual signals in an out-of-sample walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25%, it signals that the central bank has exhausted conventional easing tools and the economy is in distress, which causes market participants to price in heightened recession risk and lowers forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return in periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% is positive or statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional mean (one-sided test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth expands corporate earnings capacity and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns; this mechanism operates continuously as economic expansion feeds into equity prices over business cycle frequencies.
failure_condition: Rank IC of year-over-year GDPC1 growth vs 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 5-year window or the mean forward return in expansion quarters is not greater than in contraction quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 400,000 annualized units, it signals a collapse in residential investment and severe economic contraction, which depresses equity prices as the housing market is a leading indicator for the broader economy.
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward return in months when HOUST < 400 is positive or not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over a 10-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is expanding and the federal funds rate is below 2%, the economy benefits from both real growth and accommodative monetary conditions, creating a synergistic positive impulse for equity prices.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive mean 20-day forward return that is at least 50 basis points higher than the unconditional mean, in a 5-year walk-forward test with at least 20 joint triggers.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE above 7%), an increase in payroll employment signals the beginning of a labor market recovery, which boosts consumer incomes and sentiment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns as the economy exits recession.
failure_condition: In periods when UNRATE > 7% and PAYEMS increases, the mean 20-day forward return is not positive or is less than the mean in non-recovery high-unemployment periods, tested over a 10-year walk-forward window.
